blockingIssues.description: Importing an encrypted key without saving its passphrase forces `passphraseStorageState` to `BLOCKED`, so the library/details surfaces show a generic Keystore-invalidated repair message instead of a truthful intentionally-unsaved, non-ready state.

Replacing an encrypted imported key without persisting the new passphrase preserves the previous encrypted passphrase blob, allowing reload to promote the replaced identity back to `AVAILABLE` and silently reuse stale passphrase material.

The targeted legacy-PEM timeout diagnostic was not reproducible from a clean emulator in the reviewed commit because `/data/local/tmp/aterm-fixture-client_key_legacy_pem` was not provisioned there, and its required follow-up library note overstated changes not present in that diff.

Runtime PEM detection in `SshSessionCoordinator` accepts a narrower header set than the import/repair path, so encrypted EC/DSA PEM identities can import successfully but miss the runtime PEM loader in saved-host/session flows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature changes imported-key authentication across identity import/repair and runtime session flows, require a parity check or shared helper covering the accepted PEM header families on both sides, plus regression coverage for at least one non-RSA PEM form when the import path advertises broader support.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `encrypted-imported-key-host-session-truthfulness` review found that `ImportedKeyImportService` accepts RSA/EC/DSA/PKCS#8 PEM headers while `SshSessionCoordinator` only routes RSA/PKCS#8-looking keys through the runtime PEM loader, allowing import/runtime support to drift apart.

rejectedObservations.observation: The timeout-diagnosis review noted that `.factory/library/encrypted-imported-key-host-session-truthfulness-followup.md` described fixture-prep and coordinator/session changes not present in commit `1840b96`.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as an immediate shared-state update because the current branch already contains later cumulative changes matching that note, so the observation is historical-to-that-commit rather than a clear present-state library correction.
